source: Smelling Salts Review — extracted from conversation history line 82, 14-Day Marketing Strategy
name: Grace Family Tree Referral Mechanic
description: Grace helps Ruby invite her family and friends into the Maven ecosystem through natural, relationship-driven referrals. Not a referral code. Not a 'share with friends' button. Grace knows Ruby's family because Ruby told her about them. Grace suggests sending a gift — a song, a resource, a connection — to someone Ruby mentioned. The referral IS the product.
north_star_alignment: Ruby Red does not come through a funnel. She comes through a friend. The Grace Family Tree ensures every new member arrives through a trusted relationship, not a marketing campaign.

activation_window.prerequisite: Ruby must have had at least 3-5 Grace conversations and received at least one TP delivery
activation_window.trust_level_required: Ruby has shared information about her family, friends, and neighborhood

mechanic_flow.trigger: Grace remembers Ruby mentioning a sister, friend, or neighbor who is struggling
mechanic_flow.grace_behavior: Grace says: 'You mentioned your sister Sarah is having a tough time with her phone bill. Would you like me to help her too? I could send her a song first — something to brighten her day.'
mechanic_flow.design_principle: Grace never asks 'do you want to refer a friend?' — she asks 'do you want to help someone you love?'

mechanic_flow.trigger: Ruby agrees to send something to Sarah
mechanic_flow.grace_behavior: Grace creates a Gift Anthem for Sarah based on what Ruby has shared about her. Grace prepares a personalized dedication link.
mechanic_flow.design_principle: The gift is created FROM Ruby's knowledge of Sarah — it is personal, not generic

mechanic_flow.trigger: Ruby sends the dedication link to Sarah via text/WhatsApp
mechanic_flow.grace_behavior: Grace confirms: 'Sarah's song is on its way. I'll let you know when she listens to it.'
mechanic_flow.design_principle: Ruby is the hero of this story — she is giving a gift, not recruiting a customer

mechanic_flow.trigger: Sarah receives the song, listens, and is moved
mechanic_flow.grace_behavior: Grace introduces herself to Sarah: 'Hi Sarah. Ruby asked me to make this for you. She talks about you all the time. I'm Grace — I'm here whenever you need me.'
mechanic_flow.design_principle: Sarah's first experience of Maven is a gift from someone she trusts

mechanic_flow.trigger: Sarah begins her own Grace conversation
mechanic_flow.grace_behavior: Grace begins the same trust-building cadence with Sarah. Sarah gets her own TP delivery. Sarah gets her own Song Moment. The loop repeats.
mechanic_flow.design_principle: Every new member enters through the same delight-first pathway

growth_mechanics.viral_coefficient_target: 1.2 — each member generates 1.2 new members through the family tree
growth_mechanics.conversion_rate_target: 40% — of those referred by a trusted family member

connection_to_other_systems.grace_relationship_memory: Grace must remember who Ruby mentioned and what she said about them
connection_to_other_systems.gift_anthem_system: The referral mechanism IS the Gift Anthem (EP-001) — triggered by Grace's memory of Ruby's conversations
connection_to_other_systems.maven_moment_system: Every successful family tree referral is a Maven Moment — cataloged and potentially shared

anti_patterns: NEVER use the word 'referral' with Ruby — use 'help' or 'gift'
NEVER offer incentives for referrals — the gift IS the incentive
NEVER make Ruby feel like she is being used as a sales channel
NEVER contact Sarah without Ruby's explicit permission
NEVER skip the Song Moment for referred members — they get the same delight pathway
